text:
[residual] A wrong-repo item id could later hit a card under a repo the gh token lacks write scope on, surfacing as a downstream auth failure — but no authN logic changes here.
[residual] Selecting a palimpsest-repo card would let done/finalize attempt to edit a board item outside the ditto repo's ownership boundary; a missing permission fails at the gh write, not here.
[residual] If the shared multi-repo board implies differing ownership semantics per repo, that is unmodeled — pre-existing, not introduced here.
[residual] The absent-repository SKIP is silent (continue), so there is no trail of why an item was skipped — overlaps observability (relevant).
[residual] Two concurrent work invocations on the same WI file could race the project_item_id write, but that is pre-existing and untouched.
[residual] An unusually large board payload lengthens the linear scan, but the fetch and loop both predate this change.
[residual] A crafted repository URL could mis-normalize and cause a wrong/no match — a correctness effect covered by boundary-edge, not an exploit.
[residual] A regex mis-parse yields a wrong coord comparison (data-integrity, relevant), not code execution.
[residual] None identified.
[residual] None identified.
[residual] None identified.
[residual] Work items linked before this fix keep a possibly-wrong persisted project_item_id (no backfill) — a data-integrity concern (relevant), not a rollout-ordering one.
[residual] parseRemoteUrlToRepo was authored for git remote url output; reusing it on a Projects-API repository URL could mis-normalize if the shape differs — overlaps boundary-edge.
[residual] A differently-scoped project config is unmodeled, though it does not break the number+repo match.
[residual] None identified.
[residual] A more-frequent null result makes completion reflection silently skip the board update — covered by failure-recovery/observability, not a liveness stall.
[residual] That reason now also fires when repository is missing — a misleading diagnostic, which is an observability concern (relevant), not a directive-fidelity loss.
[close_reason] Pure in-memory matching over an already-fetched gh payload (parseBoardPosition:1381); no credential/token/login/session code is added or changed — gh auth is the untouched shell-out.
[close_reason] No permission/scope/role check exists on this path; the fix changes which item is selected, not whether the actor may act.
[close_reason] The change introduces no actor/permission model and no policy about who may claim/finalize — it is a coordinate-matching correctness fix.
[close_reason] No audit log, event record, or trail is written or altered; parseBoardPosition returns a value, it does not record.
[close_reason] Single-writer CLI over an in-memory array loop (work.ts:1383-1398); no shared mutable state, async interleaving, or lock added; the payload is fetched synchronously before the scan.
[close_reason] Bounded single linear pass over the existing items array; no new allocation, recursion, unbounded loop, or fetch beyond the already-present projectItemList call.
[close_reason] No user-supplied input; the repository URL is read from the trusted gh payload and regex-parsed, never executed or reflected. canonicalizeRepo (:210) already defends against a hostile repo name and the change tightens, not loosens, matching.
[close_reason] The repository field is regex-parsed for string comparison only; no shell/SQL/path/template sink is fed from it, and the gh args (owner/number) come from config, not the payload.
[close_reason] No secret/token/credential is read, logged, or embedded; the payload carries public owner/repo/issue coordinates and PVTI_ item ids only.
[close_reason] Data handled is repo coordinates and Project item ids, not personal data; nothing new is logged or emitted.
[close_reason] Internal dev-tooling board-matching fix; no regulated data class, consent, or retention obligation is in scope.
[close_reason] No migration, schema/version bu
…[truncated]
